concepts.title: DNA Replication and Transcription are not the same
concepts.description: DNA replication is the process of making an exact copy of DNA, whereas transcription is the process of creating a complementary RNA copy from a DNA template.

concepts.title: Central Dogma of Molecular Biology
concepts.description: DNA → RNA → Protein, the central dogma describes the flow of genetic information from DNA to proteins.

concepts.title: RNA Splicing
concepts.description: RNA splicing is the process of removing introns and joining exons in a primary transcript to form a mature messenger RNA.

concepts.title: DNA Structure
concepts.description: Mnemonics: 'Double Helix, Nitrogenous Bases, Acidic Sugar, Phosphate', or 'Double Helix, Nucleotides, Adenine, Cytosine, Guanine, Thymine, Acidic Sugar, Phosphate'.

concepts.title: Difference between Frameshift and Point Mutation
concepts.description: Frameshift mutation occurs due to insertion or deletion of nucleotides in a multiple of three, resulting in a completely different protein sequence. Point mutation occurs due to the substitution of a single nucleotide, resulting in a change in a single amino acid.

concepts.title: Genetic Code
concepts.description: The genetic code is a set of rules that translate nucleotide sequences into amino acid sequences. It consists of 64 possible codons, each coding for 20 amino acids.

concepts.title: DNA Mismatch Repair
concepts.description: DNA mismatch repair is a process that corrects errors in DNA replication and recombination by recognizing and repairing mismatched bases.

concepts.category: One of the Most Important Conceptual Questions in the Chapter
concepts.title: Why is DNA replication semi-conservative?
concepts.description: DNA replication is semi-conservative because it retains one parental strand and synthesizes a new complementary strand.
